pageProps.body:
Bates et al (2013)

Bates et al is the first (and only) paper which establishes specified conditions on how large structural breaks can be allowed to be, without affecting the consistency of PCA. As such, it can be viewed in general as an attempt to see how far the original Bai and Ng regularity conditions can be loosened.

Note however, that this paper was produced before the development of the equivalent representation theorem, and as such is less useful is determining the precise behaviour of what happens in the presence of a structural break.

They achieve this by focusing on the error term in the usual DFM model setup:

Xit=λit′Ft+eit,X_{it} = \lambda_{it}' F_t + e_{it},Xit​=λit′​Ft​+eit​,

where λit\lambda_{it}λit​ is allowed to be possibly time varying. The authors re-parameterize the cumulative drift in loadings as

Λt−Λ0=hNTξt,\Lambda_t - \Lambda_0 = h_{NT} \xi_t,Λt​−Λ0​=hNT​ξt​,

where hNTh_{NT}hNT​ is a deterministic scalar that may depend on NNN and/or TTT. Subbing this back into the general setup yields:

Xt=ΛtFt=Λ0Ft+et+hNTξtFt=Λ0Ft+et+wtX_t = \Lambda_t Ft = \Lambda_0 F_t + e_t + h_{NT}\xi_t F_t = \Lambda_0 F_t + e_t + w_tXt​=Λt​Ft=Λ0​Ft​+et​+hNT​ξt​Ft​=Λ0​Ft​+et​+wt​

Bates et al focus on three specific examples which are thought to be of particular interest.

White noise: all ξit\xi_{it}ξit​ are i.i.d. across iii and ttt, and Λt\Lambda_tΛt​ is simple Λ0\Lambda_0Λ0​ plus some uncorrelated noise.

Random walk: The ξit\xi_{it}ξit​ are each random walk processes, and as such the factor loadings each evolve over time as uncorrelated random walks.

Single large break: The ξt\xi_tξt​ is simply a sudden shift parameter:

ξt={0for regime 1Δfor regime 2,\xi_t = \begin{cases} 0 \quad \text{for regime 1} \\ \Delta \quad \text{for regime 2}, \end{cases}ξt​={0for regime 1Δfor regime 2,​

where Δ\DeltaΔ is the shift parameter. Note that this case corresponds exactly to the type of break considered by Breitung and Eickmeier, which can be retroactively classified as being observationally equivalent to the emergence of r new factors, and hence results in a a DFM with double the number of factors.

The precise assumptions required on hNTξth_{NT} \xi_thNT​ξt​ are detailed below. Notation here is quite overwhelming: i,ji, ji,j denote cross sectional indices, s,ts, ts,t denote time indices, p,qp, qp,q denote factor indices.

There exist envelope functions Q1(N,T),Q2(N,T),Q3(N,T)Q_1(N, T), Q_2(N, T), Q_3(N, T)Q1​(N,T),Q2​(N,T),Q3​(N,T) such that for all N,TN, TN,T and factor indices p1,q1,p2,q2,…rp_1, q_1, p_2, q_2, \dots rp1​,q1​,p2​,q2​,…r:

  1. sups,t≤T∑i,j=1N∣E(ξisp1,ξjtq1,Fsp1,Ftq1)∣≤Q1(N,T)sup_{s, t \leq T} \sum_{i, j = 1}^{N} |E(\xi_{isp_1}, \xi_{jtq_1}, F_{sp_1}, F_{tq_1})| \leq Q_1(N, T)sups,t≤T​∑i,j=1N​∣E(ξisp1​​,ξjtq1​​,Fsp1​​,Ftq1​​)∣≤Q1​(N,T)

  2. sups,t=1∑i,j=1N∑i,j=1N∣E(ξisp1,ξjsq1,Fsp1,Fsq1,Ftp2,Ftq2)∣≤Q2(N,T)sup_{s, t = 1} \sum_{i, j = 1}^{N} \sum_{i, j = 1}^{N} |E(\xi_{isp_1}, \xi_{jsq_1}, F_{sp_1}, F_{sq_1}, F_{tp_2}, F_{tq_2})| \leq Q_2(N, T)sups,t=1​∑i,j=1N​∑i,j=1N​∣E(ξisp1​​,ξjsq1​​,Fsp1​​,Fsq1​​,Ftp2​​,Ftq2​​)∣≤Q2​(N,T)

  3. sups,t=1∑i,j=1N∑i,j=1N∣E(ξisp1,ξjsq1,Fsp1,Fsq1,Ftp2,Ftq2)∣≤Q2(N,T)sup_{s, t = 1} \sum_{i, j = 1}^{N} \sum_{i, j = 1}^{N} |E(\xi_{isp_1}, \xi_{jsq_1}, F_{sp_1}, F_{sq_1}, F_{tp_2}, F_{tq_2})| \leq Q_2(N, T)sups,t=1​∑i,j=1N​∑i,j=1N​∣E(ξisp1​​,ξjsq1​​,Fsp1​​,Fsq1​​,Ftp2​​,Ftq2​​)∣≤Q2​(N,T)

Baltagi et al

Not the focus of their paper, but Baltagi et al re-visit Bates et al's conditions and are able to subsequently loosen it further a bit.

noteIndex.title: PhD Homepage
noteIndex.body:
Welcome to my website!

This website has been built using Dendron, and each topic has been collated into collapsible sections on the left hand side. Below is a high level overview showing how each section is connected for ease of navigation.



Dendron has incredibly powerful search functionality due to good indexing, even outside of working in raw .md files. Simply use the search bar at the top of the website to find something.

## Quick Links

[[roadmap]]

[[Meeting]]

[[TODO]]

## To do List

Type up Bai and Ng assumptions

Investigate Bates et al Assumptions

Investigate consistency of using an estimated break point in conjunction with BNIC
This seems to be consistent using k_tilde that itself may be Op(1)

Fix up BP test for better interface (low priority)

Implement simulated Andrews exp and mean Wald/LM tests

- look into RSpectra package (supposedly faster eigen)
- not sure whether to rename this to SFM or not (lots of legacy code that needs to be changed)

Data
Fix and clean data for 
- AUMD (on hold, need to find date index)
- FREDQD
- CANMD (should be straightforward)
- CANQD
- UKMD (should be straightforward)

Investigate further sources of time series that could be used during COVID

Implement truly dynamic factor models (Forni) using Dynamic PCA
- BIG task
- read into dynamic PCA
- read into testing no. of dynamic factors
- do not believe there is a literature focusing on breaks in the dynamic setup
- implement dynamic PCA
- implement testing no of factors, (look into ICshocks functions and make this interface better)
- implement testing no of factors using AW test

Implement BKW standard test (non sequential)

Fix up BKW test
- implement a general plot method which plots *something* on the y axis, but most importantly, date on the x axis with estimated break dates, and each partition labelled with the no. of factors as determined by some consistent estimator

Fix up BE test
- so that there is a consistent interface for accessing this
